mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2014 payroll growth at 159540 thousand persons
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with steady labor market expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth months produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.0% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on future equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated policy rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strengthening consumer demand and construction activity that boosts corporate revenues and profit expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to move together; isolated strength in one series is insufficient.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 732010 million dollars and HOUST at 1307 thousand units as of April 2014
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand acceleration
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints that reduce corporate output capacity and earnings potential, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices growth downward.
regime_hint: supply-constrained environment with declining industrial momentum
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in < 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 2.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) signals the Federal Reserve will maintain or tighten monetary policy, compressing equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 323.364 and FEDFUNDS at 4.22% as of April-May 2014
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure with monetary tightening response
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as corporate earnings expectations decline.
regime_hint: labor market weakness with rising joblessness
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of instances when UNRATE > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate profit growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23770.976 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q4 2013
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with above-trend growth
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both declining month-over-month signals synchronized supply and labor weakness that constrains economic output and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to weaken together; isolated weakness in one series is insufficient.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < -0.1% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change < 50 thousand persons
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.9203 and PAYEMS at 159540 thousand as of April 2014
regime_hint: synchronized economic slowdown with supply and labor constraints
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
